title: ctenopteris sikkimensis
intro: Ctenopteris sikkimensis, also known as Sikkim maidenhair, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Ctenopteris sikkimensis is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, to harness its therapeutic properties.
